solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    char repeatedCharacter(string s) {
        bool seen[26] = {false};
        for (char c : s) {
            if (seen[c - 'a']) return c;
            seen[c - 'a'] = true;
        }
        return 'a'; // This line is unreachable due to problem constraints.
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a boolean array to track characters that have been seen once. As we iterate through the string, the first character that has already been marked as seen is returned. This approach is efficient with constant space and linear time.
